feature: loop-carried physical accumulators for the AMD ISA backend (revisit N5A register accumulators)
core_insight: Treat a loop-carried accumulator EXACTLY like the existing fixed system registers (KARG s0:1, workgroup-id s2/s3/s4) already referenced directly in lowerings: a RESERVED physical VGPR that the virtual-register linear-scan regalloc NEVER sees (not tuple-tagged) and NEVER allocates (reserved out of the pool). This clears all 3 N5A walls WITHOUT weakening the single-def invariant for ordinary virtual registers, because the accumulator is simply not a virtual register.

answers.representation: A small reserved pool ACCUM_POOL = v240..v255 (16 regs), removed from the normal AMD VGPR pool (VBASE[1:]) so _vpool excludes it. Each loop-carried accumulator ELEMENT = ONE pinned physical VGPR (a SIMD VGPR holds per-lane values, so one VGPR = one per-thread accumulator across the wave's 32 lanes; the 4 warps each have their own wave-private VGPRs => one VGPR == the full per-(warp,lane) accumulator, NO per-lane register explosion). New AMDOps: ACCUM_INIT (v_mov v[pin], imm), ACCUM_UPDATE (v_<op> v[pin], v[pin], delta_vreg -- in-place RMW), ACCUM_READ (v_mov vvirt, v[pin] -- copy out after loop). The pinned index is encoded in the AMDOp arg, NOT a tuple tag.
answers.ownership: AMD-LOCAL (backend-only). No generic regalloc concept; reuses the existing fixed-physical-register pattern (KARG/workgroup-id are already fixed Regs emitted directly in lower_inst). Generic promotion deferred unless the abstraction proves clean.
answers.defs_uses: ACCUM_UPDATE/ACCUM_INIT WRITE the pinned reg; ACCUM_UPDATE/ACCUM_READ READ it. These are FIXED-register operands (real Reg v[240+k]) so the SCHEDULER and WAITCNT passes see them and order RAW/WAW/WAR correctly, BUT they carry no virtual tuple-tag so LinearScanRegallocContext never enters them into live_range (no def tracking, no allocation). The delta inputs ARE normal tracked virtual regs.
answers.live_range: The pinned reg is implicitly live everywhere (reserved), so the loop live-in/out machinery (regalloc.py:90-108) and the single-def assert (lr[v][0]==i) never apply -- it is not a tracked v. Correct loop carry is guaranteed by (a) emitting ACCUM_INIT before the RANGE (dependency/AFTER ordering into the loop preheader), (b) ACCUM_UPDATE inside the loop body, (c) ACCUM_READ after END. The scheduler treats the pinned Reg as a normal reg operand => it serializes the in-place RMW chain and won't hoist the update past its delta or reorder two writes.
answers.interference: ACCUM_POOL (v240..v255) is removed from VBASE so _vpool(ctx) returns v1..v239 only -> normal regalloc can never assign a virtual to a pinned reg. Distinct accumulator elements get distinct pinned indices (assigned at isel from a per-kernel counter, capped at pool size; overflow -> fall back to LDS).
answers.initialization: ACCUM_INIT emitted at the accumulator's .set(init) site; ordered before the enclosing loop via the existing AFTER/range ordering (same placement the LDS init uses today, just lowered to v_mov v[pin],imm instead of ds_store).
answers.final_use: After END, ACCUM_READ lowers to v_mov vvirt, v[pin]; vvirt is a normal virtual reg the store/combine consumes. (Or the consumer reads v[pin] directly if it is itself a fixed-reg-aware op.)
answers.moves: REAL v_mov_b32 for init (v[pin]<-imm) and final read (vvirt<-v[pin]); the per-iteration update is an in-place 3-address VALU (v_add/v_fma v[pin],v[pin],delta) -- NO reliance on AMDOps.MOV (the coalescing no-op). This is N5A wall #3 resolved.
answers.fallback: AMD_ISA_REG_ACCUM=0 (DEFAULT) -> the existing Ops.DEFINE_REG LDS path (amd.py:_lds_byte_offset, elf.py REG group-segment sizing) is byte-identical and unchanged. The pinned path activates only when the flag is set AND the accumulator qualifies: per-thread scalar element, COMPILE-TIME index, total elements <= pool size; otherwise fall back to LDS (conservative).

clears_n5a_walls.wall1_single_def_assert: accumulator write is NOT tuple-tagged -> never enters live_range -> lr[v][0]==i never evaluated for it (invariant untouched for real virtual regs)
clears_n5a_walls.wall2_fixed_reg_operand: ACCUM_READ/UPDATE reference v[pin] as a fixed Reg in lower_inst (exactly like _S[2] workgroup-id / KARG), not via alloc_vregs tagging
clears_n5a_walls.wall3_move_and_loop_carry: explicit v_mov_b32 for init/read + in-place v_add for update; pinned reg implicitly live across RANGE/END (reserved, untracked)

stop_conditions_respected: single-def invariant NOT weakened for ordinary vregs
pinned regs NOT in the normal allocation pool
per-thread = ONE SIMD VGPR with per-lane values (not 128 regs)
no tile-algorithm rewrite (DEFINE_REG access points are re-lowered, the tile UOp graph is unchanged)
risks: scheduler/waitcnt must SEE v[pin] as a real Reg operand for correct RMW ordering (it does -- _inst_regs extracts Reg operands) while regalloc must NOT allocate it (reserved) -- these are compatible
ACCUM_READ after a loop whose body is EXEC-masked: ensure the read is outside the masked region
pool overflow (>16 accumulator elements) -> LDS fallback
